commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c336ac778d38f67f59698e7e5c267f5836e90d91)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds structured reply metadata to `CurrentInboundPromptContext`, renders it before existing inbound context text, and adds focused prompt-prelude/runtime-context tests.

PR surface: Source +131, Tests +159. Total +290 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible but not live-reproduced here: current main has no structured reply field and still prefixes `params.currentInboundContext` into both session and model prompts. The PR body exercises the helper path with real modules, but not a live Telegram update.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the concrete blocker depends on sequencing or integrating the related runtime-context PR plus live Telegram proof, not a small isolated repair on this branch.

target.title: fix: carry reply metadata into runtime context
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a first-class current-turn reply metadata payload to `CurrentInboundPromptContext` so reply/quote target facts survive the reply prompt/runtime handoff as structured OpenClaw metadata instead of only as channel-rendered prompt text.

This ports the useful shape from a local Morty repro patch, with two cleanup changes:

- separates `replyTargetPresent` from `quotePresent`, so a normal replied-to message body is not mislabeled as a selected quote
- preserves reply metadata even when the text context prefix is empty

The trusted metadata block includes current message id, thread/topic id, reply target ids, target/quote/chain presence booleans, and reply-chain message ids. It does not include replied-to body text in the trusted metadata block or verbose trace.

## Related

Addresses the runtime handoff portion of #88032.

Complements #89428. That PR keeps inbound metadata in the runtime context channel more broadly; this PR adds the reply-specific contract that lets Telegram/forum reply facts survive as structured metadata.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A Telegram/forum reply can have `ReplyToId` / `ReplyChain` facts in channel context, but the runtime path needs a structured current-turn reply contract so the model can reliably tell which prior message is being referenced.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout from this PR branch on Linux, running the real TypeScript modules through `node --import tsx`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Built a finalized Telegram group/forum-style inbound context with current message id `34974`, topic id `777`, reply target id `34971`, and one reply-chain entry; passed that context through the real `buildReplyPromptEnvelope(...)` prompt-prelude path; rendered the real runtime current-turn prefix with `buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix(...)`; checked that structured reply metadata appears before untrusted context and that the trusted reply metadata block does not include the replied-to body text.

```bash
PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node --import tsx -e 'const { finalizeInboundContext } = await import("./src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-context.ts"); const { buildReplyPromptEnvelope } = await import("./src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.ts"); const { buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix } = await import("./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts"); const sessionCtx = finalizeInboundContext({ Body: "Sean, answer this old reply", BodyStripped: "Sean, answer this old reply", Provider: "telegram", Surface: "telegram", OriginatingChannel: "telegram", ChatType: "group", MessageSid: "34974", MessageThreadId: 777, ReplyToId: "34971", ReplyToBody: "old target body", ReplyToSender: "Alice", ReplyChain: [{ messageId: "34971", threadId: "777", sender: "Alice", body: "old target body" }] }); const envelope = buildReplyPromptEnvelope({ ctx: sessionCtx, sessionCtx, baseBody: "Sean, answer this old reply", prefixedBody: "Sean, answer this old reply", hasUserBody: true, inboundUserContext: "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=34971", isBareSessionReset: false, startupAction: "new" }); const prefix = buildCurrentInboundPromptContextPrefix(envelope.currentInboundContext); console.log(JSON.stringify({ reply: envelope.currentInboundContext?.reply, text: envelope.currentInboundContext?.text, metadataBeforeUntrusted: prefix.indexOf("Current reply metadata") >= 0 && prefix.indexOf("Current reply metadata") < prefix.indexOf("Conversation info"), metadataLeaksReplyBody: prefix.slice(0, prefix.indexOf("Conversation info")).includes("old target body") }, null, 2));'
```

**Evidence after fix:** Console output from the real module command above:

```json
{
  "reply": {
    "currentMessageId": "34974",
    "threadId": "777",
    "replyToId": "34971",
    "replyTargetPresent": true,
    "quotePresent": false,
    "replyChainPresent": true,
    "replyChainMessageIds": [
      "34971"
    ]
  },
  "text": "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\nreply_to_id=34971",
  "metadataBeforeUntrusted": true,
  "metadataLeaksReplyBody": false
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** Reply target metadata is structured, rendered before untrusted context, and the trusted reply metadata block does not leak the replied-to body text.

**What was not tested:** A live Telegram bot round-trip against an actual forum-group reply.

## Tests

- `PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/root/projects/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.test.ts` - passed 2 Vitest shards, 31 tests
- `git diff --check` - passed
